title: Use official compress/gzip implementation for gzip
body:
Podman currently uses [klauspost/pgzip](https://github.com/klauspost/pgzip), which hasn’t seen a commit in over three years. I encountered an issue pulling an image in Podman due to a rare checksum bug in pgzip that I still couldn’t fully track down. This wouldn’t occur if Podman used the official and well-tested compress/gzip implementation.

If parallelism is desired, users could install [pigz](https://github.com/madler/pigz) as an optional dependency. It also doesn't look very actively maintained, but users can uninstall it if it proves unstable.

I recommend switching Podman to use `compress/gzip`.

comments.body:
Thanks for reaching out.

This is … not much of an actionable report. In general, we can’t very well completely replace an implementation on a vague “there is an issue, I couldn’t trace it down and I’m providing a reproducer; we’d be replacing 2 components a week, and doing nothing else.

(Also, a dependency on a separately-installed binary is fairly unattractive.)

Gzip is a stable format, so no activity in a project is not obviously problematic. If there is a bug, let’s find it and fix it.

>we can’t very well completely replace an implementation

klauspost/pgzip markets itself as an "drop-in replacement" for compress/gzip. this should be a matter of find and replace.

>Also, a dependency on a separately-installed binary is fairly unattractive.

[compress/gzip](https://pkg.go.dev/compress/gzip) is part of go standard library.

>If there is a bug, let’s find it and fix it.

Is podman a project to beta test reimplementations of standard library packages?

I’m not interested in arguing hypotheticals, and I will be doing no implementation changes without an actionable bug report.

Sure, we might end up deciding that the current implementation is unviable long-term, and change things, but not on a basis of _nothing specific_.

Please file a standard Podman bug report with the usual steps to reproduce / actual results / expected results. Then there will be something to talk about.
I already reported this upstream klauspost/pgzip/issues/61
